topic: AG2 (AutoGen v0.4) Guide: Event-Driven Multi-Agent Framework for Python Developers

key_points: Introduces AutoGen v0.4 API with layered architecture: Core, AgentChat, Extensions
Covers asynchronous messaging and event-driven patterns
Explains transition from v0.2 to v0.4 breaking changes
Compares AG2/AutoGen with CrewAI and LangGraph
AG2 excels at multi-party conversational workflows
GroupChat as primary orchestration pattern with selector logic
AG2 best for group debates, consensus-building, sequential dialogues
Medium learning curve compared to CrewAI (lower) and LangGraph (similar)
Production readiness maturing with AG2 Beta rewrite
Comprehensive guide covering AG2, AutoGen, and Semantic Kernel
Multi-provider LLM support: OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Ollama
Practical guide for building multi-agent automation workflows

stats.fact: AG2 (formerly AutoGen) accumulated over 54,000 GitHub stars and millions of downloads before transitioning to the ag2ai organization in November 2024
AutoGen community had 20,000+ Discord members, 3,000+ GitHub forks, and 100,000+ monthly PyPI downloads as of 2024
AG2 requires Python 3.10 to 3.13 and supports multi-provider LLMs including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, Alibaba DashScope, and Ollama
AG2 v0.4 was a complete redesign focused on improved code quality, robustness, usability, and scalability with async-first architecture
A 4-agent GroupChat with 5 rounds generates at least 20 LLM calls, making cost management critical for high-volume applications

angles: Complete migration guide from AutoGen v0.2 to AG2/AutoGen v0.4 with breaking changes
Event-driven vs request/response patterns: when to use each in multi-agent systems
AG2 vs LangGraph vs CrewAI: practical decision guide for Python developers in 2026
Building production-ready AG2 systems: cost optimization, state persistence, and observability
AG2 Beta (autogen.beta) preview: what's coming in v1.0 and streaming architecture
Real-world use cases: code generation pipelines, document review, research automation with AG2
